text:
MIRAGES & TWISTS
OF MATHEMATICS
WITH AI
textPreview: MIRAGES & TWISTS | OF MATHEMATICS | WITH AI

text: A technical, evidence-first judgement of what changed—and what did not.
textPreview: A technical, evidence-first judgement of what changed—and what did not.

text: Bartosz Naskręcki · UAM / CCAI PW · x.com/nasqret
textPreview: Bartosz Naskręcki · UAM / CCAI PW · x.com/nasqret

text:
Pacing: 60 seconds.
Open with the conclusion: the capability jump is real, but the strongest public narrative often confuses a correct answer, a checked proof, a new theorem, and a new theory. This lecture separates those four achievements.

The capability jump is real.
The popular theory of it is not.
textPreview: The capability jump is real. | The popular theory of it is not.

text:
• long tool-using runs now work
• proof assistants close real gaps
• agents produce durable artifacts
• some open problems have fallen
textPreview: • long tool-using runs now work | • proof assistants close real gaps | • agents produce durable artifacts | • some open problems have fallen

text:
• one benchmark = a profession
• fluent prose = mathematical proof
• isolated success = autonomy
• scale alone = new abstraction
textPreview: • one benchmark = a profession | • fluent prose = mathematical proof | • isolated success = autonomy | • scale alone = new abstraction

text:
Pacing: 65 seconds.
State the two-sided judgement plainly. The talk is neither a dismissal nor a victory lap. The useful unit of analysis is a documented workflow with a verification boundary.
Four achievements that press reports routinely collapse.
textPreview: Four achievements that press reports routinely collapse.

text: a final value or candidate object
textPreview: a final value or candidate object

text: an argument checked by experts or a kernel
textPreview: an argument checked by experts or a kernel

text: new concepts, structure, and reusable explanation
textPreview: new concepts, structure, and reusable explanation

text: The frontier rises sharply from 1 to 4.
textPreview: The frontier rises sharply from 1 to 4.

text:
Pacing: 70 seconds.
Use this vocabulary throughout the talk. A formal proof can establish correctness relative to a statement while saying little about novelty, importance, or whether the statement captured the intended mathematics.
Capability, evaluation, and governance all moved at once.
textPreview: Capability, evaluation, and governance all moved at once.

text:
Pacing: 80 seconds.
This is a chronology, not a ranking. Sources: arXiv:2601.07421; arXiv:2602.21201; OpenAI GPT-5.4 release (5 March); OpenAI unit-distance report (20 May); Epoch FrontierMath v2 (12 June); Leiden Declaration (2 June).
A remarkable campaign—and a terrible single-number benchmark.
textPreview: A remarkable campaign—and a terrible single-number benchmark.

text:
88 green full-resolution records
79 unique Erdős problems
190 unique primary problems listed
commit 0f72fdb · 22 June 2026
textPreview: 88 green full-resolution records | 79 unique Erdős problems | 190 unique primary problems listed | commit 0f72fdb · 22 June 2026

text:
selection is adaptive
literature is discovered later
problem statements can be wrong
obscurity is not difficulty
textPreview: selection is adaptive | literature is discovered later | problem statements can be wrong | obscurity is not difficulty

text:
Pacing: 85 seconds.
Be exact about the denominator. I parsed the primary categories 1a–1d of the community-maintained wiki at commit 0f72fdb. The wiki itself says this is not a benchmark. Count records and unique problems separately because one problem can have multiple contributions.
The blueprint case: proposal, formal search, ratification.
textPreview: The blueprint case: proposal, formal search, ratification.

text:
• GPT-5.2 Pro proposed the strategy
• Aristotle produced a Lean proof
• experts inspected the mathematics
• comparable literature surfaced later
textPreview: • GPT-5.2 Pro proposed the strategy | • Aristotle produced a Lean proof | • experts inspected the mathematics | • comparable literature s...

text:
• exact statement
• kernel-checkable artifact
• public paper and code
• provenance is discussable
textPreview: • exact statement | • kernel-checkable artifact | • public paper and code | • provenance is discussable

text:
Pacing: 80 seconds.
The lesson is the workflow, not the marketing adjective 'autonomous'. The public record is unusually good: arXiv:2601.07421 and the Erdős wiki include the statement, proof route, and later literature context.
Unit distances: original structure, not merely closure.
textPreview: Unit distances: original structure, not merely closure.

text:
• n^(1+δ) construction
• algebraic number theory enters geometry
• external mathematicians checked it
• a general-purpose reasoning model
textPreview: • n^(1+δ) construction | • algebraic number theory enters geometry | • external mathematicians checked it | • a general-purpose reasoning...

text:
• release the full proof record
• separate vendor and independent claims
• track reproducibility and compute
• wait for ordinary publication review
textPreview: • release the full proof record | • separate vendor and independent claims | • track reproducibility and compute | • wait for ordinary pu...

text:
Pacing: 85 seconds.
OpenAI's 20 May report calls this the first autonomous solution of a prominent central open problem. That is a provider claim, though the proof was checked by external mathematicians and companion remarks are public. The methodological novelty is the cross-domain construction. Source: openai.com/index/model-disproves-discrete-geometry-conjecture/.

A campaign is not a benchmark.
A benchmark is not mathematics.
textPreview: A campaign is not a benchmark. | A benchmark is not mathematics.

text:
adaptive problem choice
iterative human attention
open literature search
credit for partial progress
textPreview: adaptive problem choice | iterative human attention | open literature search | credit for partial progress

text:
fixed sampling rule
frozen task versions
predefined budget
auditable scoring
textPreview: fixed sampling rule | frozen task versions | predefined budget | auditable scoring

text: THEORY BUILDING IS COMPLEMENTARY TO BOTH
textPreview: THEORY BUILDING IS COMPLEMENTARY TO BOTH

text:
Pacing: 70 seconds.
This distinction is central. Campaigns measure what a motivated team can accomplish. Benchmarks estimate performance under a protocol. Mathematics asks a third question: what structure did we learn?
The strongest trust boundary is still a small kernel.
textPreview: The strongest trust boundary is still a small kernel.

text: Statement extraction is now the critical specification step.
textPreview: Statement extraction is now the critical specification step.

text:
Pacing: 75 seconds.
Reuse the formalization slides from Falenty and EML, but sharpen the claim. Lean does not validate the informal intention automatically. It validates the formal term against the formal statement and trusted environment.
Correctness becomes local, explicit, and replayable.
textPreview: Correctness becomes local, explicit, and replayable.

text:
type-correct proof term
explicit dependencies
reproducible replay
textPreview: type-correct proof term | explicit dependencies | reproducible replay

text:
wrong theorem formalized
hidden assumptions
definition mismatch
textPreview: wrong theorem formalized | hidden assumptions | definition mismatch

text:
novelty and importance
explanatory value
credit and authorship
textPreview: novelty and importance | explanatory value | credit and authorship

text: Formal verification narrows the error surface. It does not erase judgement.
textPreview: Formal verification narrows the error surface. It does not erase judgement.

text: TRUST THE KERNEL; AUDIT THE STATEMENT
textPreview: TRUST THE KERNEL; AUDIT THE STATEMENT

text:
Pacing: 75 seconds.
Give a concrete Falenty example: a tiny De Morgan theorem has a complete proof term; the pedagogical Erdős #728 file contains sorry and is correctly labelled a sketch. That contrast is pedagogically valuable.
Proof search is accelerating. Statement alignment is harder.
textPreview: Proof search is accelerating. Statement alignment is harder.

text:
• theorem-sized Lean proof search
• tactic repair and library lookup
• translating local lemmas
• checking generated terms
textPreview: • theorem-sized Lean proof search | • tactic repair and library lookup | • translating local lemmas | • checking generated terms

text:
• long informal papers
• implicit conventions
• semantic equivalence of statements
• maintainable library architecture
textPreview: • long informal papers | • implicit conventions | • semantic equivalence of statements | • maintainable library architecture

text:
Pacing: 70 seconds.
Mention Aristotle, AlphaProof, DeepSeek-Prover, Kimina, and LeanArchitect as different points in the stack. Avoid treating them as interchangeable: some prove formal statements, some translate, some orchestrate search.
A formalization factory, not a single generated proof.
textPreview: A formalization factory, not a single generated proof.

text:
calculator primitives
100 public theorems
0 sorry / 0 admit
8,062 dispatched jobs
textPreview: calculator primitives | 100 public theorems | 0 sorry / 0 admit | 8,062 dispatched jobs

text: The durable result is the compiler architecture and the audit trail.
textPreview: The durable result is the compiler architecture and the audit trail.

text:
Pacing: 90 seconds.
These numbers come from the repository README and should be read as a project snapshot. Aristotle handled proof search; GPT Pro, Claude, Codex, Mathematica, and human review had distinct roles. Source: github.com/nasqret/eml-formalization.
The first 'complete' report was not a valid formalization.
textPreview: The first 'complete' report was not a valid formalization.

text: Separate-model review found sorry, boundary abuse, and broken bridges.
textPreview: Separate-model review found sorry, boundary abuse, and broken bridges.

text: THE SECOND PAIR OF EYES CHANGED THE MATHEMATICS
textPreview: THE SECOND PAIR OF EYES CHANGED THE MATHEMATICS

text:
Pacing: 80 seconds.
This is one of the most honest results in the talk. A second model family, isolated from the first scratchpad, found real defects and forced a stronger architecture. Independent review may be the most immediately valuable AI substitute in the loop.
One brief. Three inspectable learning artifacts.
textPreview: One brief. Three inspectable learning artifacts.

text:
Choose a theorem. State assumptions.
Compile it. Expose every gap.
Explain the proof term in prose.
textPreview: Choose a theorem. State assumptions. | Compile it. Expose every gap. | Explain the proof term in prose.

text: Students can distinguish a proof, a sketch with sorry, and a polished explanation.
textPreview: Students can distinguish a proof, a sketch with sorry, and a polished explanation.

text:
Pacing: 70 seconds.
The local release includes a Lean lab, games, teaching notes, and source decks. In this repository I package two compiling Falenty examples and intentionally exclude the sorry-based Erdős sketch from the verified set.

Delegate proof search.
Retain mathematical responsibility.
textPreview: Delegate proof search. | Retain mathematical responsibility.

text:
• search Lean libraries
• synthesize proof terms
• close routine gaps
• replay at scale
textPreview: • search Lean libraries | • synthesize proof terms | • close routine gaps | • replay at scale

text:
• choose the statement
• inspect assumptions
• assess novelty
• own the published claim
textPreview: • choose the statement | • inspect assumptions | • assess novelty | • own the published claim

text: AI IS A TOOL, NOT AN AUTHOR
textPreview: AI IS A TOOL, NOT AN AUTHOR

text:
Pacing: 70 seconds.
Aristotle is prominent because it produces Lean artifacts checked by the kernel. Mention alternatives without turning the slide into a leaderboard. This principle anticipates the Leiden Declaration.
A clean example of discovery plus formal verification.
textPreview: A clean example of discovery plus formal verification.

text:
Complete classification of product intersection sets in semigroups.
New result, not only a formalized old proof.
textPreview: Complete classification of product intersection sets in semigroups. | New result, not only a formalized old proof.

text:
Autonomously discovered and verified in Lean by Aristotle.
Public preprint; named human authors own review and exposition.
textPreview: Autonomously discovered and verified in Lean by Aristotle. | Public preprint; named human authors own review and exposition.

text:
Pacing: 75 seconds.
Correct the common shorthand: the paper answers Nathanson's questions rather than a single named conjecture. Authors: van Doorn, Monticone, Tang. The abstract explicitly records autonomous discovery and Lean verification.

Not one brilliant prompt.
One controlled state machine.
textPreview: Not one brilliant prompt. | One controlled state machine.

text: Every cycle must leave a state, a test, and a reason to continue.
textPreview: Every cycle must leave a state, a test, and a reason to continue.

text:
Pacing: 75 seconds.
Define the operational unit: files, scripts, exact tests, status JSON, checkpoints, and explicit failure states. Context windows help, but durable external state is what makes a research run auditable and resumable.
Inference-time compute is now a mathematical resource.
textPreview: Inference-time compute is now a mathematical resource.

text: Report budgets, retries, tools, and human interventions—not only the winner.
textPreview: Report budgets, retries, tools, and human interventions—not only the winner.

text:
Pacing: 75 seconds.
FrontierMath Tier 4 allows up to one million tokens and Python tools. Agent papers increasingly compare systems at different test-time compute. A score without a budget is not a scientific comparison.
An agent improved a real benchmark through verified engineering.
textPreview: An agent improved a real benchmark through verified engineering.

text:
Q×T score
1,170 peak qubits
1,434,070 Toffoli
9,024 trusted shots
textPreview: Q×T score | 1,170 peak qubits | 1,434,070 Toffoli | 9,024 trusted shots

text: The 84.3% reduction is reproducible challenge evidence—not a proof of all-input correctness.
textPreview: The 84.3% reduction is reproducible challenge evidence—not a proof of all-input correctness.

text:
Pacing: 90 seconds.
Be precise about scope. This is reversible secp256k1 point addition under the challenge evaluator, not a break of ECDSA and not a complete quantum attack. The trusted registry records zero classical, phase, and ancilla failures over 9,024 shots.
Campaign logic can outperform one-shot problem solving.
textPreview: Campaign logic can outperform one-shot problem solving.

text: 51 accepted distinct pairs in the 17 June repository snapshot.
textPreview: 51 accepted distinct pairs in the 17 June repository snapshot.

text:
Pacing: 80 seconds.
Use only high-level totals because the competition remains open until 15 August and the repository is private. The public lesson is the protocol: generation, exact Magma verification, live-open checks, and deduplication.
Finding the counterexample was easier than finding the right theorem.
textPreview: Finding the counterexample was easier than finding the right theorem.

text:
• diagonal-grid counterexample
• fast computational exploration
• exposed a false formulation
• generated local evidence
textPreview: • diagonal-grid counterexample | • fast computational exploration | • exposed a false formulation | • generated local evidence

text:
• identify the structural defect
• refine ORD → MEOD → EORD
• select the durable statement
• explain why it works
textPreview: • identify the structural defect | • refine ORD → MEOD → EORD | • select the durable statement | • explain why it works

text: COUNTEREXAMPLES ARE SEARCH; THEOREMS NEED STRUCTURE
textPreview: COUNTEREXAMPLES ARE SEARCH; THEOREMS NEED STRUCTURE

text:
Pacing: 75 seconds.
Source: github.com/nasqret/orphic-symmetry-talk-mim. This is a compact example of my main criticism: current models are strong at local search and falsification, but structural repair remains a human-led act of representation and taste.
A GPT Pro + Codex campaign broke the conjectural slope bound.
textPreview: A GPT Pro + Codex campaign broke the conjectural slope bound.

conjectural bound: 8/3
profile: (24,0,32,0)
c̄₁²=112, c̄₂=40
G=208

text: Finite-field discovery; exact QQbar reconstruction; independent SageMath and Magma certificates.
textPreview: Finite-field discovery; exact QQbar reconstruction; independent SageMath and Magma certificates.

text: WITH PIOTR POKORA · MODEL LABEL IS PROJECT-REPORTED
textPreview: WITH PIOTR POKORA · MODEL LABEL IS PROJECT-REPORTED

text:
Pacing: 105 seconds.
This is the strongest personal example. The equation is x0^4 − x0 x1^3 − x2^4 + x2 x3^3 = 0. The project records two 24-line subconfigurations. The user identifies the run as GPT-5.6; there is no public OpenAI release page for that label, so the deck treats it as project-reported rather than public product metadata.

Computation can discover the route.
The paper can still end computer-free.
textPreview: Computation can discover the route. | The paper can still end computer-free.

Gröbner bases
exact reconstruction
800 conics
machine-audited cases
textPreview: Gröbner bases | exact reconstruction | 800 conics | machine-audited cases

human lemma extraction
structural compression
computer-free proof
independent reading
textPreview: human lemma extraction | structural compression | computer-free proof | independent reading

text: COMPUTATION AS SCAFFOLDING, NOT A PERMANENT CRUTCH
textPreview: COMPUTATION AS SCAFFOLDING, NOT A PERMANENT CRUTCH

text:
Pacing: 75 seconds.
Source: github.com/nasqret/quadrics. This is a useful answer to the false dichotomy 'computer proof or human proof': tool-heavy discovery can be followed by a conceptually cleaner argument.
The amplified human arrived before the superhuman mathematician.
textPreview: The amplified human arrived before the superhuman mathematician.

text:
Naskręcki + Ono
mathematical discovery
in the age of AI
textPreview: Naskręcki + Ono | mathematical discovery | in the age of AI

text: The 2026 evidence fills the right-hand workflow; the final arrow still points to the future.
textPreview: The 2026 evidence fills the right-hand workflow; the final arrow still points to the future.

text:
Pacing: 85 seconds.
Reproduce Figure 1 from my Nature Physics paper with Ken Ono. The figure distinguishes machine-augmented discovery from a hypothetical superhuman mathematician. The intervening months validate the workflow more than the endpoint.
A strong result because the failures and outputs are inspectable.
textPreview: A strong result because the failures and outputs are inspectable.

6/10 by majority expert assessment
Problem 8 not unanimous
textPreview: 6/10 by majority expert assessment | Problem 8 not unanimous

10 unpublished research lemmas
human solutions withheld
raw prompts and outputs released
textPreview: 10 unpublished research lemmas | human solutions withheld | raw prompts and outputs released

small sample
best-of-two attempts
human grading remains essential
textPreview: small sample | best-of-two attempts | human grading remains essential

text: The important object is the disclosed experiment, not the headline percentage.
textPreview: The important object is the disclosed experiment, not the headline percentage.

text:
Pacing: 75 seconds.
Aletheia iteratively generates, verifies, and revises. The paper reports 6/10 according to majority expert assessments and explicitly notes disagreement on P8. Raw prompts and outputs are in google-deepmind/superhuman.
The product is a workbench, not an oracle.
textPreview: The product is a workbench, not an oracle.

text:
intent refinement
artifact review
uncertainty management
textPreview: intent refinement | artifact review | uncertainty management

text: This architecture is closer to research practice than single-turn evaluation.
textPreview: This architecture is closer to research practice than single-turn evaluation.

text:
Pacing: 75 seconds.
The paper also reported 23/48 on FrontierMath Tier 4 v1. That score is now superseded by the v2 benchmark audit, so discuss the architecture separately from the old leaderboard claim.
The benchmark correction is itself the most important result.
textPreview: The benchmark correction is itself the most important result.

text: 42% of all FrontierMath problems were addressed in the update.
textPreview: 42% of all FrontierMath problems were addressed in the update.

text: VERSION THE BENCHMARK, NOT ONLY THE MODEL
textPreview: VERSION THE BENCHMARK, NOT ONLY THE MODEL

text:
Pacing: 95 seconds.
Epoch says v2 corrected 123 Tier 1–3 problems and 12 Tier-4 problems, and removed 5 plus 7 respectively. The full dataset now has 338 problems, with 43 in Tier 4. Do not compare old percentages to v2 as if the task set were unchanged.

A useful case study.
Not a public benchmark datapoint.
textPreview: A useful case study. | Not a public benchmark datapoint.

text:
sustained technical attempt
local tools + calculations
candidate solution produced
strategy weakness exposed
textPreview: sustained technical attempt | local tools + calculations | candidate solution produced | strategy weakness exposed

text:
private task
held-out reference
no complete evaluation trace
confidential benchmark details
textPreview: private task | held-out reference | no complete evaluation trace | confidential benchmark details

text:
Pacing: 80 seconds.
This slide uses my retained project memory and session record, not a public source. The honest message is methodological: long context and tools made the attempt qualitatively different, but privacy prevents turning it into a reproducible public claim.
A score is a claim about a protocol, not intelligence in general.
textPreview: A score is a claim about a protocol, not intelligence in general.

text: Then publish raw traces where confidentiality permits.
textPreview: Then publish raw traces where confidentiality permits.

text:
Pacing: 75 seconds.
A benchmark can still be valuable after correction if it records its failure transparently. The governance failure would be hiding the correction or continuing to quote obsolete scores.
The least speculative success is building rich learning environments.
textPreview: The least speculative success is building rich learning environments.

text:
memory + plans
Git workflows
research artifacts
textPreview: memory + plans | Git workflows | research artifacts

text: Agentic tools make coherent course ecosystems feasible for one instructor.
textPreview: Agentic tools make coherent course ecosystems feasible for one instructor.

text:
Pacing: 80 seconds.
These numbers come from the live course landing pages. The cryptanalysis course advertises 45 chapters in 15 parts; the neural-network course advertises 42 chapters, 329 slides, and 22 applets. Treat this as a production case, not a controlled learning-outcomes study.
One research brief. A durable project, not a disposable answer.
textPreview: One research brief. A durable project, not a disposable answer.

text:
Bring a real task, source files, and a success criterion.
End with a repository that another person can inspect.
textPreview: Bring a real task, source files, and a success criterion. | End with a repository that another person can inspect.

text: Participants built substantial discipline-specific code; the strongest gains came from iteration and verification.
textPreview: Participants built substantial discipline-specific code; the strongest gains came from iteration and verification.

text:
Pacing: 75 seconds.
This is my teaching experience, not a randomized trial. Emphasize what is observable: participants produced working repositories, documentation, and tests. Avoid converting enthusiasm into an unsupported causal claim about learning.
A protocol for serious mathematical use
textPreview: A protocol for serious mathematical use

text: SPECIFY — write the exact claim and evidence bar
textPreview: SPECIFY — write the exact claim and evidence bar

text: SEARCH — let agents explore variants and literature
textPreview: SEARCH — let agents explore variants and literature

text: CHECK — compile, test, and use independent tools
textPreview: CHECK — compile, test, and use independent tools

text: CRITIQUE — isolate a second reviewer and attack assumptions
textPreview: CRITIQUE — isolate a second reviewer and attack assumptions

text: The mathematician remains able to explain, defend, and extend the result.
textPreview: The mathematician remains able to explain, defend, and extend the result.

text:
Pacing: 75 seconds.
This is the practical recommendation. The exact tooling can change next month; the protocol survives model churn.

Local brilliance is common.
Global mathematical control is not.
textPreview: Local brilliance is common. | Global mathematical control is not.

text:
• code and exact computation
• library-scale proof search
• counterexample generation
• recombining known techniques
textPreview: • code and exact computation | • library-scale proof search | • counterexample generation | • recombining known techniques

text:
• choosing the right representation
• sustaining long strategic coherence
• inventing reusable abstractions
• knowing what is worth proving
textPreview: • choosing the right representation | • sustaining long strategic coherence | • inventing reusable abstractions | • knowing what is worth...

text:
Pacing: 80 seconds.
This is my critical judgement from the case studies. Current systems can produce real novelty, but the modal pattern is powerful recombination plus search. Structural creativity is rarer and harder to recognize.

many variants
cheap falsification
strong local moves
textPreview: many variants | cheap falsification | strong local moves

text:
transfer known ideas
combine tools
repair local gaps
textPreview: transfer known ideas | combine tools | repair local gaps

text:
invent the invariant
reframe the problem
build a reusable theory
textPreview: invent the invariant | reframe the problem | build a reusable theory

text: World models, latent-space reasoning, and better memory may help—but that is a research agenda, not today's result.
textPreview: World models, latent-space reasoning, and better memory may help—but that is a research agenda, not today's result.

text:
Pacing: 85 seconds.
Avoid claiming an architectural impossibility. Say that present LLM-centred systems remain inconsistent at representation change and theory building. World models or latent reasoning may alter this, but the evidence is not here yet.
Keep mathematical governance while capability accelerates.
textPreview: Keep mathematical governance while capability accelerates.

text: Policies should preserve autonomy without freezing methods.
textPreview: Policies should preserve autonomy without freezing methods.

text:
Pacing: 80 seconds.
The Declaration was published 2 June 2026, reflects practice as of May, and is endorsed by the IMU. I am one of the authors and a website convenor. Its principles are scientific infrastructure, not anti-technology sentiment.
More verified artifacts; uneven progress on theory.
textPreview: More verified artifacts; uneven progress on theory.

text:
selective theory gains
not general autonomy
textPreview: selective theory gains | not general autonomy

text: Expect discontinuous examples and slow institutional adaptation.
textPreview: Expect discontinuous examples and slow institutional adaptation.

text:
Pacing: 75 seconds.
The safest forecast is architectural: more stateful agents, proof assistants, and disclosed traces. The riskiest claim is universal autonomous theory building; do not make it.

DELEGATE SEARCH.
RETAIN JUDGEMENT.
PUBLISH THE TRACE.
textPreview: DELEGATE SEARCH. | RETAIN JUDGEMENT. | PUBLISH THE TRACE.

text:
The right question is not whether AI can do mathematics.
It is which mathematical responsibilities we choose to automate—and how we will know that the result deserves trust.
textPreview: The right question is not whether AI can do mathematics. | It is which mathematical responsibilities we choose to automate—and how we wil...

text: x.com/nasqret · github.com/nasqret · full notes and artifacts: GitHub Pages
textPreview: x.com/nasqret · github.com/nasqret · full notes and artifacts: GitHub Pages

text:
Pacing: 55 seconds.
End on responsibility rather than replacement. Invite the audience to challenge the evidence taxonomy, the benchmark methodology, and the forecast.
